description_long: This is a dark blue, twin-propeller military airplane with a compact, rounded fuselage and a distinctive mid-wing configuration. The wings are straight and slightly swept, with two large propellers mounted at the front of each wing and a prominent star insignia on the upper surface, indicating U.S. military origin. The tail features a unique H-tail design with twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer connecting them. The landing gear is extended, with two main wheels visible beneath the wings and a smaller nose wheel under the cockpit area. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, enclosed with a rounded canopy. The overall design is streamlined, with minimal surface detailing and a matte finish.
description: A dark blue, twin-propeller military airplane with a mid-wing, H-tail design and U.S. star insignia. Features an enclosed forward cockpit and extended landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the twin propellers, mid-mounted wings, H-tail, and U.S. star insignia on the upper wing.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the symmetry of the wings and propellers.
description_view_2: Angled rear-side view displaying the landing gear, propellers, and the streamlined fuselage with tail configuration.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the twin propellers, rounded cockpit canopy, and the wide stance of the landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with slight sweep, no winglets, and minimal dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines, tractor configuration, mounted on the leading edge of each wing.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed and extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a rounded canopy, likely single-seat.
